javascript.subDescription: Master JavaScript from fundamentals to advanced concepts. Build dynamic, interactive, and performant web applications.

phases.title: The Magic Behind the Screen: Logic & Action
theory: The Big Three: Skeleton (HTML), Skin (CSS), and the Muscle (JS)
The 10-Day Miracle: Where JS came from and why it's the 'King of the Browser'
The Live Translator: How your browser reads code (Interpreter vs. Compiler)
The Turbocharger: What is an 'Engine' (V8) and how JIT 'Supercharges' your code
JS vs. The Rest: Why learning JS first is like learning the 'Universal Language' of the web
practicals: Console Hijacking: Open the 'Inspect' tool and change the color of a live website
Secret Messages: Write your first `console.log` and see the browser 'talk back'
Brainstorming Action: List 5 things on Amazon.com that use JS (e.g., 'Add to Cart' button)

games.title: WarriorJS
games.description: A fun way to understand how simple logic (Action) controls everything.

phases.title: The Rules of the Game: Data & Typing
theory: Understanding Data: Information is more than just 'Text'
Liquid Containers: What 'Dynamic Typing' means (JS adapts to what you give it)
The Primitive Box (Single Items): Numbers, Text (Strings), Booleans (Yes/No)
The Empty Boxes: Understanding `null` (Intentional empty) and `undefined` (Missing)
The Lunchbox (Non-Primitives): Objects and Arrays—storing 'Groups' of things
practicals: The `typeof` Lab: Use the browser to reveal what 'Shape' a piece of data actually is
The Empty Box Test: Create a variable but don't give it a value to see `undefined` in action
Primitive vs. Object: Change a number vs changing a 'List' and notice the difference (Mutability)

phases.title: The Shape Shifter: Type Casting & Coercion
theory: Implicit Casting: When JS is 'Too Helpful' (e.g., Why `1 + '1'` is '11' instead of 2)
Taking the Reins (Explicit): Using `Number()` and `String()` to force the shape
The Equality Trap: Strict (`===`) vs. Loose (`==`) comparisons
Truthiness: Why nearly everything is 'True' until it's 'Empty' (Falsy values)
Input Cleaning: Why we must always 'Cast' data coming from a User's screen
practicals: The Calculator Bug: Fix a program that adds numbers incorrectly because of String concatenation
The Truthy Riddles: Predict if a variable (like an empty string or the number 0) is true or false
Strict Check: Refactor your old code to use `===` to avoid invisible bugs

phases.title: Labeled Storage: Variables & Memory
theory: What is a Variable? (Labeling a box on a shelf)
Storing vs. Recalling: How JS remembers what you told it
The 'Unchangeable' rule: Why `const` is your best friend
The 'Switch' rule: When to use `let` for things that change
Naming Conventions: Writing code that humans can read (CamelCase)
Memory Basics: Why variables 'live' and 'die' (Introduction to Scope boundaries)
practicals: The Locker Room: Create multiple variables for a user profile (Name, Age, IsLoggedIn) and update them
The Const Trap: Try to change a `const` and see the browser 'Protect' the data with an error
Project: 'The Personal Vault' - Build a script that stores and displays sensitive user info using correct labels

phases.title: Decision Making: Operators & Control Flow
theory: Conditional Logic: The 'If/Then' mindset of coding
Comparison Operators: Checking for equality and size (>, <, ===)
Logical Operators: Combining conditions (AND, OR, NOT)
The Switch Statement: A better way to handle multiple 'Cases'
The Ternary Operator: Code shorthand for 'Either / Or' choices
practicals: The Gatekeeper: Write a script that checks if a user is old enough to enter a site
The Grade Calculator: Take a number and output a Letter Grade based on ranges
Project: 'The ATM Simulation' - Create a logic flow for withdrawing money, checking balance, and handling 'Insufficient Funds'

games.title: WarriorJS
games.description: Navigate your hero through floor after floor using 'If' statements to survive.

phases.title: The Repeaters: Loops & Iteration
theory: The Problem: Doing things over and over (Manual Labor)
The Solution: Loops (The Digital Assembly Line)
The 'While' Loop: 'Keep going while this is true'
The 'For' Loop: 'Do this exact number of times'
Iterating through Collections: Looking into every 'Lunchbox' item
practicals: The Countdown: Build a loop that counts down from 10 to launch
The List Reader: Create a script that prints out every item in your shopping list
Project: 'The Multiplication Table' - Using nested loops to create a math helper tool

phases.title: The Action Mindset: Functions Basics
theory: What is a Function? (A Saved Recipe for later)
Input vs. Output: Arguments (Ingredients) and Return (The Meal)
Declarations vs. Expressions: Two ways to write a recipe
Reusable Logic: Why we never 'Copy-Paste' code
practicals: The Greeting Machine: A function that takes a name and says 'Hello'
The Math Helper: Create functions for `add`, `subtract`, and `multiply`
Project: 'The Simple Budgeter' - A set of functions to calculate total expenses and remaining balance

phases.title: Arrow Functions: The Fast Shortcut
theory: Regular vs. Arrow: Writing less but doing more
The 'One-Liner': Implicit returns (Short and Sweet)
The 'This' Problem: Why arrows are easier for beginners
When NOT to use Arrows: Keeping some rules in mind
practicals: The Refactor: Take 5 old 'Recipe' functions and rewrite them as modern Arrows
The Math One-Liner: Create arrows for square, cube, and percentage calculation
Project: 'The Modern Converter' - A set of arrow functions for currency and weight

phases.title: Data Structures - Part 1: Advanced Arrays & Strings
theory: The Modern Array: `map()`, `filter()`, and `reduce()` (The 'Big Three')
Searching the Crowd: `find()`, `includes()`, and `findIndex()`
Advanced Strings: `slice()`, `replace()`, and `split()`
Template Literals: Dynamic text without the mess
practicals: The VIP List: Filter a list of users to find only those over 18
The Price Tag: Use `map` to add a '$' sign to every number in an array
The Name Cleaner: Take a messy user input and use string methods to fix capitalization and spacing
Project: 'The Smart Inventory' - A system to find, update, and remove items from a store list

phases.title: The Digital Profile: Mastering Objects
theory: Objects as Profiles: Storing related data together (Name: 'John', Role: 'User')
Property Access: Dot (`.`) vs. Bracket (`[]`) notation
Object Methods: Giving your profiles 'Skills' (Action inside Data)
Looping through Profiles: `Object.keys()` and `Object.values()`
practicals: The RPG Character: Build a character object with health, strength, and an 'Attack' skill
The Dynamic Key: Create a script that accesses a property based on what the user types
Project: 'The Social Media Bio' - A system that displays a user's name, followers, and interests

phases.title: The Unpacker: Destructuring & Spread
theory: Destructuring: Taking only what you need from a big box
The Spread Operator (`...`): Pouring one box into another
Default Values: What happens if the box is empty?
Merging Data: Combining two user profiles into one
practicals: The Specific Grab: Extract just the 'Email' and 'Username' from a massive object
The Array Merge: Combine a 'Home' list and 'Work' list using the `...` operator
Project: 'The Profile Updater' - A function that takes an old profile and merges new updates into it

phases.title: Specialized Lists: Maps & Sets
theory: Sets: The 'Unique' rule (No duplicates allowed)
Maps: The 'Super Object' (When keys aren't just text)
When to use what: Array vs. Object vs. Map vs. Set
Iteration: How to loop through these modern collections
practicals: The Duplicate Destroyer: Take a list of items with repeats and use a Set to get only uniques
The ID Map: Store user data where the User ID (a number) is the key
Project: 'The Unique Tag System' - A blog tag system that ensures no duplicate categories are added

phases.title: Changing the Page: DOM Basics
theory: The DOM: The browser's map of your HTML
Selectors: Finding elements by ID, Class, or Tag
Changing Content: `innerHTML` vs. `textContent`
The Appearance: Changing CSS styles via JavaScript
practicals: The Color Picker: Create a button that changes the background of the entire page
The Dynamic List: Add a new `<li>` to an existing list without refreshing
Project: 'The Personal Greeting' - An input field that updates an H1 tag live as you type

phases.title: Making it Interactive: Clicks & Keys
theory: Event Listeners: Telling the browser to 'Listen' for an action
Click Events: Handling button presses
Keyboard Events: What happens when you type (The `event.key`)
Input Validation: Preventing bad data before it's sent
practicals: The Password Toggle: Build a 'Show Password' checkbox that changes the input type
The Click Counter: A button that tracks how many times it's been clicked
Project: 'The Simple Todo List' - Add items to a list and clear them with a button

phases.title: The Ripple Effect: Event Propagation
theory: The Stadium Wave: Understanding Event Bubbling
Stopping the Wave: `event.stopPropagation()`
Delegation: One parent handling 100 children (The 'Mall Security' analogy)
Default Actions: Why some clicks (like links) need to be stopped
practicals: The Bubbling Test: Create nested boxes and see how one click triggers three alerts
The Smart List: Use one listener on a `<ul>` to handle clicks on 50 different `<li>` items
Project: 'The Interactive Gallery' - A grid of images where clicking one shows it in a 'Master' view

phases.title: The Memory Bank: Storage & JSON
theory: LocalStorage: Storing data that stays even after you close the browser
JSON: The universal language for data (Stringify and Parse)
SessionStorage: Temporary memory for the current tab
Security: Why we never store passwords in the browser
practicals: The Theme Saver: Save the user's Dark/Light mode preference so it persists
The Object Saver: Convert a user object to JSON and save it in the browser
Project: 'The Persistent Todo App' - A list that reloads your items even if you refresh the page

phases.title: The Alarm Clock: Timers & Intervals
theory: setTimeout: 'Wait 3 seconds, then do this'
setInterval: 'Do this every 5 seconds forever'
Cleanup: Stopping timers with `clearTimeout` and `clearInterval`
practicals: The Delayed Alert: Show a 'Welcome' message 2 seconds after the page loads
The Countdown: Build a timer that counts down from 60 to 0
Project: 'The Digital Clock' - A live clock that updates every second

phases.title: The Waiting Game: Callbacks & Order
theory: Synchronous vs. Asynchronous: Waiting for the coffee to brew
Callbacks: The 'Don't call us, we'll call you' rule
Execution Order: Why some code runs while other code waits
The Callback Hell: Why too many nested recipes are a bad thing
practicals: The Order Test: Use logs to see which code finishes first in an async sequence
The Recipe chain: A function that calls another function after a delay
Project: 'The Image Loader' - Simulate loading an image and showing a message only when it's done

phases.title: The Digital Agreement: Promises
theory: What is a Promise? (A ticket for a future result)
The Three States: Pending, Kept (Resolved), and Broken (Rejected)
The Chain: Using `.then()` and `.catch()` to handle results
Combining Efforts: `Promise.all()` for multi-tasking
practicals: The Coin Flip: A promise that resolves if 'Heads' and rejects if 'Tails'
The Parallel Fetch: Wait for three simulated 'Data Packages' to arrive at once
Project: 'The Weather Simulator' - A chain of promises that 'checks' the sky and then 'predicts' rain

phases.title: Modern Waiting: Async/Await
theory: Async/Await: Making async code look like a simple list of instructions
The `await` keyword: Pausing the function without freezing the browser
Error Handling: The `try...catch` block (The safety net)
When to pause vs. when to run in parallel
practicals: Promise Refactor: Take a `.then()` chain and rewrite it with `await`
The Safety Net: Build a function that handles a 'failed' promise gracefully
Project: 'The Data Dashboard' - An async function that gathers stats from three places and displays them

phases.title: The News Reporter: Fetch & APIs
theory: What is an API? (The waiter at the restaurant)
Fetch: Asking the internet for information
HTTP Methods: GET (Reading) vs. POST (Submitting)
CORS: Why some websites won't talk to you (The gatekeeper)
practicals: The User Feed: Fetch a list of random users from a public API and display them
The Image Finder: Fetch a random dog/cat photo on every button click
Project: 'The Live Crypto Tracker' - A small app that shows the real-time price of Bitcoin using a public API

phases.title: The Family Tree: Prototypes & Inheritance
theory: Prototypes: The hidden link between objects
The Chain: How JS searches for a property
Built-in Prototypes: Why all Arrays have `push()` and `pop()`
Inheritance: Creating a 'Parent' and 'Child' relationship without complex syntax
practicals: The Prototype Peek: Use `__proto__` to see the 'Parent' of an array in the console
The Custom Method: Add a new 'Skill' to the global String prototype (e.g., `yell()`)
Project: 'The Animal Kingdom' - Create a parent 'Animal' object and multiple 'Child' animals that inherit its traits

phases.title: The Digital Blueprint: Classes
theory: Classes: The factory for creating objects
The Constructor: Setting up the initial state
Extends & Super: Building on top of existing blueprints
Getters & Setters: Protecting your data with smart access
practicals: The User Factory: Build a `User` class that takes a name and email
The Admin Upgrade: Create an `Admin` class that extends `User` with extra permissions
Project: 'The Car Showroom' - Build a class system for different types of cars (EV, Sports, Truck) with shared properties

phases.title: Who Am I? - This & Bind
theory: The `this` Keyword: Why it's the most confusing thing in JS
Context: Why `this` changes depending on who called the function
Bind, Call, and Apply: Taking manual control of the context
Arrow Functions vs Regular: The final showdown on scope
practicals: The Lost Context: Create an object method that 'breaks' when passed as a callback, then fix it with `.bind()`
Method Borrowing: Use `.call()` to make one object use another object's function
Project: 'The Smart Button' - A class-based button handler that keeps its context even when clicked

phases.title: The Safety Net: Error Handling
theory: Try/Catch: Catching bugs before they crash the page
The Finally Block: Code that runs no matter what
Throwing Errors: Creating your own custom warning signs
Async Errors: Catching bugs inside Promises and Fetch
practicals: The Crash Test: Intentionally write broken code and use `try/catch` to keep the app running
The Custom Validator: Throw an error if a user enters a password that's too short
Project: 'The Robust News Feed' - A fetch app that shows a friendly error message if the API is down

phases.title: Professional Building: Design Patterns
theory: Why Patterns? Writing code that other developers can understand
The Module Pattern: Keeping your variables private (The 'Safe Room')
The Factory Pattern: Creating complex objects without showing the mess
The Observer Pattern: How apps 'React' to changes (Intro to the React mindset)
practicals: The Private Vault: Use an IIFE to create a variable that cannot be accessed from the console
The Shape Factory: Create a function that returns different object types based on input
Project: 'The Notification System' - A simple pub-sub system where one action triggers multiple updates

phases.title: The Traffic Cop: The Event Loop
theory: The Single Thread: Why JS can only do one thing at a time
The Call Stack: Tracking where we are in the code
The Task Queue: Where async code waits its turn
Microtasks vs Macrotasks: Why Promises have priority over Timers
practicals: Stack Overflow: Intentionally crash the call stack with infinite recursion to see the limit
The Priority Race: Predict the order of logs between a `setTimeout` and a `Promise.resolve()`
Project: 'The Execution Visualizer' - Build a small log-based tool that proves the Event Loop order

phases.title: The Locked Room: Closures
theory: Lexical Scope: How functions remember their birthplace
Closures: Functions that carry their surrounding variables with them
Private State: Creating variables that only one function can touch
Memory Pitfalls: Avoiding leaks with closures
practicals: The Secret Counter: A function that returns a counter that no one else can reset
The Partial Recipe: A function that 'pre-fills' some arguments (Currying intro)
Project: 'The Secure Bank Account' - A closure-based object that stores a balance and only allows access through specific functions

phases.title: The Janitor: Memory & GC
theory: Memory Life Cycle: Allocate, Use, Release
Garbage Collection: How JS cleans up the mess for you
References: Why deleting one variable might not free up memory
Leaks: Common ways developers accidentally clog up the browser
practicals: The Reference Trap: Create two variables pointing to the same object and see what happens when you null one
The Leak Hunter: Identify a common leak (like a forgotten setInterval) and fix it
Project: 'The Memory Monitor' - Research and document how to use the Chrome 'Memory' tab to find leaks

phases.title: The Shadow: Proxy & Reflect
theory: Proxies: Placing a 'Guard' in front of your object
Traps: Intercepting reads, writes, and deletions
Reflect: The modern way to perform object operations
Practical Uses: Validation, Logging, and Reactive Data
practicals: The Validation Guard: Create a Proxy that refuses to set an age younger than 0
The Logger: Build a Proxy that logs every time a property is accessed
Project: 'The Smart Data Binder' - A simple system where changing an object automatically updates a piece of UI text

phases.title: The Pattern Hunter: Regular Expressions
theory: Regex: The secret code for searching text
Patterns: Matching emails, phone numbers, and dates
Flags: Global, Case-insensitive, and Multi-line
Methods: `test()`, `match()`, and `replace()` with Regex
practicals: The Email Checker: Write a Regex that validates if a string is a real email
The Censorship Tool: Use Regex to find and replace 'Bad Words' in a paragraph
Project: 'The Form Guardian' - A complete form validator using Regex for Username, Email, and Password

phases.title: The Detective: Debugging Mastery
theory: Beyond `console.log`: Professional debugging tools
Breakpoints: Pausing time to see exactly what's happening
Stepping: Running code one single line at a time
Watch Expressions: Monitoring a specific variable as it changes
practicals: The Bug Hunt: I give you a broken script, you use a breakpoint to find the exact line it fails
The Stack Trace: Use the debugger to see how 5 different functions called each other
Project: 'The Debugger Logbook' - A guide on using the 'Network', 'Source', and 'Console' tabs together

phases.title: Master Challenges: The Logic Games
practicals: FizzBuzz 2.0: The classic challenge with a modern twist
Palindrome Checker: Logic without using built-in string methods
The Missing Number: Find the missing item in an unsorted list
Array Chunking: Split a big array into smaller pieces

phases.title: Master Challenges: Data Wizardry
practicals: The Deep Comparison: Check if two big nested objects are identical
The Flattening: Conver a massive nested array into one flat list
Anagram Finder: Check if two words use the exact same letters
Frequency Map: Count how many times every word appears in a book

phases.title: Capstone Projects: Engineering to Win
practicals: Project 1: 'The ATM 2.0' - A complete CLI app with accounts, transfers, and error handling
Project 2: 'The Smart Shopping Cart' - A persistent system with discounts, tax, and local storage
Project 3: 'The Weather Dashboard' - A clean, interactive UI that fetches live data and displays it beautifully
Project 4: 'The Personal Finance Tracker' - Track expenses and draw a simple 'Bar Chart' using standard JS

games.title: Elevator Saga
games.description: The ultimate test of your logic and efficiency.
